headlines.description: — Josh Giddey had 26 points, 11 assists and seven rebounds, Zach Collins added 16 points off the bench and the Chicago Bulls erased a 13-point, second-half deficit to beat the Charlotte Hornets 129-126 on Friday night and snap a seven-game losing streak.
headlines.shortLinkText: Josh Giddey scores 26 points, Bulls hang on to beat Hornets 129-126 to snap 7-game losing streak

headlines.description: — Isaiah Stewart scored 17 points to lead a balanced scoring attack as the Detroit Pistons beat the Atlanta Hawks 142-115 on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pistons tie NBA record for scoring balance in 142-115 win over Hawks

headlines.description: — Joel Embiid scored a season-high 39 points, Paul George had 23 and the Philadelphia 76ers beat the Indiana Pacers 115-105 on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Embiid scores season-high 39 points to lead 76ers to 115-105 win against Pacers

headlines.description: — Donovan Mitchell scored 24 of his 48 points in the fourth quarter, leading the Cleveland Cavaliers back from a 15-point deficit in the final period to a 130-126 victory over the Washington Wizards on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Mitchell scores 24 of his 48 points in the 4th quarter to rally Cavaliers past Wizards 130-126

headlines.description: — Keyonte George scored a career-high 39 points, including the final six points for Utah, as the Jazz beat Memphis 130-126 on Friday night in Ja Morant's return to the Grizzlies' lineup.
headlines.shortLinkText: Keyonte George scores a career-high 39 points as Jazz beat Grizzlies 130-126

headlines.description: — Anthony Davis scored 20 of his 24 points in the second half to go with 14 rebounds and three blocks, and Cooper Flagg had 22 points and eight assists as the Dallas Mavericks rallied to beat the Brooklyn Nets 119-111 on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Anthony Davis has 24 points and 14 rebounds to lead Mavericks past Nets 119-111

events.name: Minnesota Timberwolves at Golden State Warriors

headlines.description: — Rudy Gobert had 24 points and 14 rebounds, Donte DiVincenzo made a clutch 3-pointer with 28 seconds remaining and the Minnesota Timberwolves beat the Golden State Warriors 127-120 on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Curry scores 39 in return but Timberwolves top Warriors 127-120 without Edwards
